rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Protective Order Relieving 973 Market Association Llc'S Obligation To Respond To Deft The Chris James Corp., James Nunemacher, X-Deft'S Leo Cassidy, Deirdre Cassidy, Lj Sf Investments Llc, 973 Market St Llc'S Judicial Council Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Admissions, Set One
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, Line1, PLAINTIFF 973 MARKET ASSOCIATES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED's Mtn For Protective Order Relieving 973 Market Association Llc'S Obligation To Respond To Deft The Chris James Corp., James Nunemacher, X-Deft'S Leo Cassidy, Deirdre Cassidy, Lj Sf Investments Llc, 973 Market St Llc'S Judicial Council Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Admissions, Set One Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for a Protective Order is Denied. The discovery propounded include contention interrogatories and seek documents and admissions that each cross-defendant is entitled to receive pursuant to C.C.P. §§2030.010, 2031.010 and 2033.010, et al. If the discovery responses are the same for each of the cross-defendants as the moving papers contend, it would be just as easy to provide the same response in each set as in the first set with verifications from each separate cross-defendant. The moving party will have 60 days from the date of the order to provide responses to the discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANTs JOSEPH CASSIDY and CENTRIX BUILDERS, INC. Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 2, 3, 4, and 8. Those causes of action describe claims that belong to 973 Market Association, LLC aka the Buyer LLC. The cross-complainants did not have an individual contract with Mr. Cassidy/Centrix and there is no basis for equitable indemnity because plaintiff is not bringing a tort action and there is no joint and several liability. The court denies the motion for judgment on the pleadings as to causes of action 7 and 9 (fraud and violation of the UCL) because cross-complaints sufficiently allege individual harm that is not derivative from the Buyer LLC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BISHOP VS. CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED GENERAL DENIAL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JASON BISHOP's DEMURRER TO AMENDED GENERAL DENIAL. Overruled. Defendant has pled sufficient facts with the requisite particularity to allege the first, third - eighth affirmative defenses. The Court's ruling on Defendant's demurrer to the Complaint does not preclude Defendant from asserting different or contradictory facts to the Complaint. A lthough Bus. & Prof. Code § 17529.5 is a strict liability statute, Defendant may reduce its liability. (See Hypertouch, Inc. v. ValueClick, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 822.) A demurrer to a prayer for relief for attorney fees is improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BISHOP VS. CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Consumertracks Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JASON BISHOP's Motion To Strike Consumertracks Amended Answer. Denied as to the first and fourth affirmative defenses. Granted, without leave to amend, as to the prayer for attorney fees. The motion to strike the first and fourth affirmative defenses is predicated on the same grounds as the demurrer that has been overruled. Defendant has not pled the statutory basis for attorney fees, and has not demonstrated that amendment is proper. To the extent that Defendant may seek relief for attorney fees pursuant to CCP § 128.7, Defendant may file a motion for sanctions; it need not be sought in the Answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: THE PRESRAY CORPORATION, A NEW YORK CORPORATION VS. N. BARNARD ALDRIDGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTs N. ALDRIDGE, SEBASTOPOL INDUSTRIAL PARK LLC, HIGHWAY PARTNERS LLC's Motion For Change Of Venue. Granted. The case is transferred to Sonoma County. Defendants have met their burden in showing that venue is not proper in San Francisco under any statutory grounds. The moving papers establish that the contracts attached to the complaint were entered into and to be performed in Sonoma County. (Complaint, Ex. C.) Defendants presented evidence in reply to rebut Plaintiff's argument that Defendant N. Barnard Aldridge resides in San Francisco. (Declaration of N. Bernard Aldridge, par. 5.) Defendants also presented evidence in reply to rebut Plaintiff's argument that the entity defendants are located in San Francisco. (Declaration of Jeffrey S. Lyons, Exs. A-C.) The evidence establishes that all Defendants reside or have their principal place of business in Sonoma County. Defendants' request for an award of fees pursuant to CCP sec. 396b(b) is denied. Defendants do not address this request in their memorandum of points and authorities. Further, no evidence is presented as to "(1) whether an offer to stipulate to change of venue was reasonably made and rejected, and (2) whether the motion or selection of venue was made in good faith given the facts and law the party making the motion or selecting the venue knew or should have known." (CCP § 396b(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
